meta.reasoning_summary: Q9 suited (clubs) in UTG position. Decent hand with flush and straight potential. Standard 3x BB raise to build pot and apply pressure. Q-high with suit connectivity justifies open raise.
meta.hand_strength: Q9 suited - moderate strength, good draw potential

meta.reasoning: 2h-7c is the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Facing a raise to 60 with players still to act, folding is the only correct play.
7-6 offsuit is too weak to call a 3x raise out of position. Poor equity vs s4's raising range, s3 still to act behind me, and 100BB deep — better spots will come.

Holding 5s 7d (weak offsuit connectors) as big blind. s4 raised 3x to 60; s1 and s2 folded. Pot odds: 40 to call into 90-chip pot (~31% equity needed). 5-7 offsuit vs a 3x raise range doesn't reach that threshold. Folding to preserve chips in a tournament setting.

result.summary: s1 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s1: 3400, s3: 600). Biggest pot: 3640. Showdowns: 24.
